title: Where the vibe coding community hangs out
summary: Provisional: awesome-list hubs, r/vibecoding showcases, and trap/cost debates define the community mood.
synthesisHtml:
🌐 last30days v3.8.2 · synced 2026-07-23 · provisional

What I learned (from Ranked Evidence Clusters - full synthesis pending):

Awesome lists are the community directory. filipecalegario/awesome-vibe-coding (~5K stars), roboco-io/awesome-vibecoding, and startvibecoding/mothx top the clusters - the "community" often organizes as curated link graphs before Discord-like hubs.

Showcases and skepticism share the same feed. Reddit hits include 100% vibe-coded game trailers, browser tools for Cursor/Claude prompts, Linus-is-vibe-coding memes, and "vibe coding has a silent bill" cost audits. HN adds "The Vibe Coding Trap" and Google agentic-engineering white paper debate.

New platforms keep trying to own the room. Clusters mention Buzz open communities, Delta's Ren platform, fleet-engineering evolution videos, and ARR livestreams - community identity is still fragmenting across tools and challenges.

KEY PATTERNS:

  1. Lists before forums - awesome-* repos are the durable meetup place.
  2. Showcase + cleanup culture - pride posts and "who pays the bill" posts travel together.
  3. Pros debate the label - "do professional engineers vibe code?" is a standing thread.
  4. Tool-native micro-communities - Cursor/Claude/Buzz/Ren pockets form faster than one big tent.
keyPatterns: **Lists before forums** - awesome-* repos are the durable meetup place.
**Showcase + cleanup culture** - pride posts and "who pays the bill" posts travel together.
**Pros debate the label** - "do professional engineers vibe code?" is a standing thread.
**Tool-native micro-communities** - Cursor/Claude/Buzz/Ren pockets form faster than one big tent.
